signals.reasoning: This is a fresh SOL short rather than the older failed one, so I would manage it as a hold for now instead of auto-covering it immediately. The position is only modestly underwater, the veto is now clear, and the board finally allows the bearish thesis some room to work, though confidence stays capped because SOL itself still has broadly constructive trend support and there is still no stop with liquidation distance under 1%.

BTC still shows a broadly trend-supported rebound and is not offering a clean fresh short. At the same time, after the large recovery move I would not force a fresh long from here in a position-management round.

ETH remains structurally firm, with price and open interest both higher and no obvious bearish failure. That makes it a poor short candidate and not a cleaner action than simply managing the live SOL position.
GRIFFAIN looks more like a completed long-liquidation flush than an early fresh short. I would rather avoid chasing that after the damage is already done.
ZEREBRO has upside momentum but not enough supporting open-interest quality to trust for a fresh long, and it is not a clean short either. It stays watch-only.

CC still has squeeze-style support from negative funding, but the move is already underway and the open-interest history is incomplete. It is interesting, but not cleaner than the live SOL management decision.

LIT is the clearest secondary short on the board because price is slightly down while open interest is still building, which points to fresh shorts pressing rather than a completed flush. Confidence is moderate because the overall market still has residual squeeze energy.
TST remains an active squeeze continuation name with very large price and open-interest expansion, which makes it too crowded to chase and too unstable to fade. It stays on the sidelines.
RESOLV has some constructive continuation structure, but the move is not unique enough to justify action while a live position is already on the book. Watch-only.
LDO is still strong on raw structure, but it is already extended after the rebound and does not outrank the active SOL short. That keeps it as a pass rather than a fresh long.
KNEIRO has upside momentum, but the supporting structure is too incomplete and too stretched to justify fresh risk. It stays watch-only.
